sentences.text: Yes, Happy Friday to you as well.
It's been busy per usual, but good.
Busy is always a good thing.
Thanks for taking a few minutes this morning.
I actually was recently assigned as an account executive on your account.
Simply just like wanted to introduce myself.
And just kind of do a check in.
I'm not sure when the last check in with Silverlake was so just.
It's been a it's been a minute.
Okay, well, that's that's why I'm here.
See how things were going on your end.
Introduce myself and see if you had any questions.
Thoughts on how things are going, etc.
So with that being said, it looks like it looks as if you guys have been a partner of ours since February of last year.
How are things going for you?
So I'll tell you, we specialize in the senior housing market for HUD low income seniors.
So 95 of our sites, if not closure.
Are that demographic where we have very few multifamily sites.
But everything we have listed with you guys right now that's active is because we're still in a lease up phase with them.
I only take the sites off after we've leased up, and I see that their wait list is growing, and we're starting to cut back that marketing spend?
I think we are on both Google and the rentable package.
It's very budget friendly, which is great.
And then, as we've acquired new sites that have new needs, I've either listed them on here or switched out, etc.
So that's kind of where we're at.
I think our portfolio is actually all the way up to date with what we need listed versus what we need taken off.
I just put in a cancellation for, I believe.
Who did I send Skyline the other day?
I think page took it so that one's going to be coming off.
But they're they're fully leased, and it's unfortunately this isn't giving us enough leads to keep it going.
So that's 1 property where we weren't getting a ton of leads at.
Have you been pretty happy with the lead flow and generation at the others?
Yeah, I think it stays pretty pretty steady for what it is, especially being such a niche market of 62 and older.
You know we do get quite a few on unqualified leads.
But that's with any Internet listing site and a senior property.
But usually, when we put our senior sites on an Internet listing site, it's because we need a ton of move ins and leads.
So it's okay to have some of those unqualified.
How how many properties are you overseeing?
We are just about to close on our 100th property.
We have multiple deals under contract.
So essentially, we just get the lease ups.
Yep, because the rest of them, like we just took on a site and they have a 3 year.
Wait list like there would be no need to spend.
And that's the majority of the properties that we're buying are these senior sites that have been stabilized for years, etc.
We take on a little bit of a problem, child, and we have to flip it.
So at at this given moment, like, you have all of the lease ups, or any properties with any sort of need on with us at right now.
Anything like from me, where you could use assistance on our end, or you being taken care of when you reach out to us.
Yeah, I think your team is very responsive.
I've had no issues with onboarding or really taking sites off.
So yeah, everything right now has been very, very smooth.
I've not had any issues that I can say on any level.
I honestly, I I know this has only been 5 min.
I don't have anything else I just wanted to check in and introduce myself.
Typically, there's something that we can help out with.
But it sounds like we're in a pretty good spot here.
And we should be good to go.
You have my email now, like, if you have any questions, obviously.
Just been on the account as well, so like we'll both be here to help you out if you have any new properties or need things swapped or so forth, so.
Thank you so much for your time today.
